name: Crooked Creek

calibration.gaugeName: Crooked Creek at Kelly Crossing at Yellville, AR
calibration.keySource: Composite feet-based key: AGFC Crooked Creek Water Trail (state agency) + OzarkAnglers paddler forum consensus, cross-referenced with Float NWA cfs guidance. NWS Little Rock lists NO official flood stage for this gauge, so high/dangerous tiers are paddler-derived, not an official flood threshold.

too_low.source: OzarkAnglers forum (paddler consensus): floatable below 10.5 ft only 'if your tolerance for dragging your kayak is high'; cross-ref Float NWA comfortable-minimum ~250 cfs. Method: below ~10.5 ft = skinny water / heavy gravel dragging = practical too-low floor.

low.source: No distinct sourced 'low-but-runnable' band. Sources treat ~10.5 ft as a sharp pivot between draggy and ideal; roughly 10.5-11.5 ft is the skinny low edge of runnable (Float NWA ~250 cfs 'comfortable minimum' falls here). Left null rather than guessed. Method: honest gap.

optimal_min.source: OzarkAnglers forum: 'ideal water level anywhere from Pyatt bridge to Yellville is around 10.5-12.0 ft on the Kellys Crossing graph.' Method: bottom of ideal float range. Note: equals too_low value because ~10.5 ft is the pivot (below = draggy, at/above = ideal).

optimal_max.source: AGFC Crooked Creek Water Trail (state agency): 'A moderate level for floating the creek is 12-12.5 feet.' OzarkAnglers forum ideal tops ~12.0 ft. Method: top of comfortable/ideal range.

high.source: OzarkAnglers forum: creek is floatable 'up to 13.0 and maybe 13.5 if you are a good kayaker.' Method: 12.5-13.5 ft is high/pushy water for experienced paddlers only.

dangerous.source: OzarkAnglers forum: '~13.5 ft maybe, if you are a good kayaker' = practical upper ceiling; above this is beyond safe recreational floating. Reinforced by AGFC/arkansas.com warnings that the creek 'can turn into a raging torrent very quickly.' NOTE: NWS Little Rock defines NO official flood stage for this gauge, so 13.5 ft is a paddler-derived ceiling, not an official flood threshold — judge high water conservatively.

calibration.notes: GAUGE CORRECTION (safety-critical, incorporated): the originally-supplied ID 07056000 is 'Buffalo River near St. Joe, AR' (drainage 829 sq mi, Newton County) — a DIFFERENT river; verified verbatim against the USGS site service. Its live values (189 cfs / 3.39 ft on 2026-07-13) coincidentally resembled the expected numbers. The correct Crooked Creek gauge is USGS 07055607 'Crooked Creek at Kelly Crossing at Yellville, AR' (drainage 398 sq mi, Marion County; reports 00060 discharge + 00065 gage height), reading 203-205 cfs / 10.75-10.76 ft on 2026-07-13. All values here are on 07055607. LADDER UNIT = FEET (gage height) because the AGFC and local paddler keys are stated in feet on this gauge's datum (moderate flow ~10.5-12.5 ft; datum cross-confirmed: NWS Yellville read 10.80 ft and USACE Yellville ~10-11 ft the same week — do NOT reuse the Buffalo River gauge's ~3.4 ft datum). CFS CROSS-REFERENCE (Float NWA, on 07055607): ideal 250-1000 cfs; comfortable-floating minimum ~250 cfs, below which expect frequent gravel dragging. LIVE SANITY CHECK: today's 205 cfs / 10.76 ft sits just below the 250 cfs / ~11 ft comfortable minimum = low but floatable with some dragging (consistent with the summer draw-down). FLASHY: entirely rain-fed, rises fast; no NWS flood stage published, so treat rapid rises and any level above ~13.5 ft as dangerous regardless.

metadata.description: Crooked Creek is a clear, gravel-bottomed Ozark stream running roughly 80 miles across Boone and Marion counties to the White River near Cotter. Designated by the Arkansas Game & Fish Commission as a Blue Ribbon Smallmouth Bass stream and a 22-mile Water Trail (Lower Pyatt to Yellville), it offers easy Class I floats past long gravel bars, riffles, fast chutes and deep pools, and is famed for feisty smallmouth bass. Entirely rain-fed and flashy, it drops low and skinny in summer and fall (and often goes dry east of Yellville) yet can rise into a dangerous torrent within hours after heavy rain.
metadata.nhdReachHint: Crooked Creek, Boone/Marion counties, AR - from headwaters near Harrison flowing generally east/northeast past Pyatt, Snow, Mark Oliver, Kelly's Slab and Yellville to the White River near Cotter. Primary floated reach: Pyatt to Yellville (~22 mi water trail).
metadata.managingAuthority: Arkansas Game and Fish Commission (AGFC) - Crooked Creek Water Trail; Fred Berry Conservation Education Center at Kelly's Slab
metadata.accessLaw: Arkansas: the public may float and wade streams capable of navigation. Use AGFC public access points (Pyatt, Snow, Mark Oliver, Kelly's Slab, Yellville City Park); the streambed and banks along stretches deemed non-navigable can be privately owned, so do not trespass above the ordinary high-water mark or portage across private land without permission.

hazards: Kelly's Slab low-water bridge: a concrete slab crossing at the Fred Berry Conservation Education Center (just west/south of Yellville). Scout and portage; can be hazardous when water flows over the slab at higher levels. AGFC explicitly advises 'use caution at Kelley's Slab.'

Flash flooding: creek is entirely rain-dependent and flashy - 'can turn into a raging torrent very quickly, especially during heavy spring rains' and become 'too dangerous to float.' Check weather/gauge before and during a trip.
No official NWS flood stage is published for this gauge, so there is no automated flood alert threshold - judge rapid rises and any stage above ~13.5 ft as dangerous.
Strainers / log jams requiring portage, plus rocky shoals, fast chutes and low-hanging tree limbs (Class I-II hazards in places).
Summer/fall low water: gravel-bar dragging when the creek drops; the reach east of Yellville toward Cotter is intermittent and often nearly dry - not a reliable float.
